removals.reason: False positive: 'Gall Bladder & Bile Duct Cancer' is gallbladder/biliary cancer, NOT urinary bladder carcinoma. Matched only on the word 'bladder' — different organ/entity (homonym trap).
Generic-noun overlap on 'inhibitors': these are antibiotic mechanism modules (cell-wall/protein synthesis inhibitors), not CYP450 enzyme inducers/inhibitors. False positive.
Generic-noun overlap on 'inhibitors': antibiotic mechanism modules, unrelated to drug-metabolizing enzyme induction/inhibition. False positive.
Generic-noun overlap on 'inhibitors': these are anticancer targeted-therapy modules (TKIs, small-molecule inhibitors), not metabolic enzyme inducers/inhibitors. False positive.

Alport Syndrome is a glomerular disease lecture matched on the generic word 'syndrome'; though hereditary, this leaf teaches renal pathology not core genetics. Better placed under glomerular diseases; likely generic-noun false positive for a genetics topic.
Malabsorption Syndrome/Celiac/IBD is an intestinal-disorders lecture matched only on the generic word 'syndrome'; it does not teach genetics. Generic-noun overlap false positive.
